entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench, the crop studied for its susceptibility to stalk rot under water stress.

entities.name: WATER DEFICIT STRESS
entities.description: Moisture deficit occurring after flowering that predisposes the crop to disease.

entities.name: STALK ROT
entities.description: A disease also known as charcoal rot, characterized by soft stalks and fungal spread.

entities.name: MACROPHOMINA PHASEOLINA
entities.description: The causal fungus of charcoal rot.

entities.name: LODGING
entities.description: The collapse of the crop stem resulting from stalk rot.

entities.name: LINE SOURCE SPRINKLER IRRIGATION
entities.description: A technique producing a gradient of water deficit stress to study quantitative relationships.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced, which decreases with increased water deficit.

entities.name: TIME OF INOCULATION
entities.description: The timing of fungal introduction used to study disease incidence.

entities.name: PERCENTAGE OF SOFT STALKS
entities.description: A parameter used to measure the spread of stalk rot.

entities.name: NUMBER OF NODES CROSSED
entities.description: A parameter used to measure the spread of stalk rot.

entities.name: LENGTH OF FUNGAL SPREAD
entities.description: A parameter used to measure the spread of stalk rot in centimeters.

entities.name: RAINFREE DRY SEASONS
entities.description: The environmental period during which the line source irrigation method is effective for study.
